headline: Core contracts are explorer-verified, but audit-to-deploy correspondence is incomplete

findings.text: The Ethereum Morpho core contract at 0xBBBBBbbBBb9cC5e90e3b3Af64bdAF62C37EEFFCb and the Base deployment at the same address both show source-code verification as exact matches; the Ethereum MetaMorphoFactory and MetaMorphoV1_1Factory pages also show exact-match verified source.

The linked morpho-blue repository identifies Morpho.sol as the core contract and the linked metamorpho repository describes MetaMorphoFactory deploying immutable MetaMorpho vault instances; these structures correspond to the explorer-visible Morpho and MetaMorphoFactory source names, but I did not perform a local compile or bytecode diff.

The morpho-blue audit directory contains OpenZeppelin and Cantina reports dated 2023-10-13, 2023-11-13, and 2024-01-05; the metamorpho audit directory contains Cantina and OpenZeppelin/Cantina periphery reports dated 2023-11-14, 2023-11-16, and 2024-01-05.

OpenZeppelin audit coverage is present for Morpho Blue and MetaMorpho/periphery, while additional Cantina reports exist but are not counted as recognized-firm coverage under this run's auditor-recognition checklist.

The deployed Morpho core source is verified, but the exact deploy commit was not pinned; the active MetaMorphoV1_1Factory verified on Etherscan uses a v1.1 contract name and compiler settings that I could not match to the linked metamorpho repository or the listed MetaMorpho audit scope.

The Morpho core deployments assessed are not shown as proxies; Etherscan displays a proxy heuristic warning on MetaMorpho factory pages, but the verified source names are factory contracts and no separate implementation address was identified from the opened pages.

steelman.red: A red case would be strongest if the current deployed MetaMorpho v1.1 factory or unassessed chain deployments were treated as main protocol contracts without a linked public repo/audit match.
steelman.orange: An orange case is strongest because the assessed contracts are verified, but current deployed-source-to-audited-commit correspondence is incomplete and the v1.1 factory was not matched to the linked repos or audit scope.
steelman.green: A green case would be strongest for the Morpho core singleton alone, because Ethereum and Base source are exact-match verified and the public morpho-blue repo plus OpenZeppelin audit coverage are available.

rationale.verdict: Choosing orange because explorer verification and public source are strong for the assessed core contracts, but I could not prove exact deployed commits or recognized-firm audit coverage for all currently relevant factory/vault code, especially MetaMorphoV1_1Factory.

evidence.shows: Ethereum Morpho core contract source is verified as Exact Match, contract name Morpho, compiler v0.8.19, deployed by contract creation, constructor owner argument shown.
Base Morpho core contract source is verified as Exact Match, contract name Morpho, compiler v0.8.19, no proxy marker found on page search.
Ethereum old MetaMorphoFactory source is verified as Exact Match, contract name MetaMorphoFactory, compiler v0.8.21, file browser includes src/MetaMorphoFactory.sol and src/MetaMorpho.sol.
Ethereum MetaMorpho Factory v1.1 source is verified as Exact Match, contract name MetaMorphoV1_1Factory, compiler v0.8.26.
Public morpho-blue repository; README describes Morpho Blue as a noncustodial lending protocol and says Morpho.sol contains most core contract source; audits folder is linked.
Public metamorpho repository; README describes MetaMorpho as ERC-4626 vaults on top of Morpho Blue and says MetaMorphoFactory deploys immutable on-chain vault instances.
Morpho Blue audit directory lists 2023-10-13 OpenZeppelin, 2023-11-13 Cantina managed review, and 2024-01-05 Cantina competition reports.
OpenZeppelin Morpho Blue audit report dated 2023-10-13; extracted report text identifies morpho-blue scope at commit bdcf70a and IRM scope at commit bdf8ee0.
Cantina managed review report for Morpho Blue dated 2023-11-13; extracted report text says review ran Sep 28 to Oct 16 on commit hash beginning 11e69b.
Cantina competition report for Morpho Blue dated 2024-01-05 is present in the linked audit directory.
MetaMorpho audit directory lists 2023-11-14 Cantina managed review, 2023-11-16 OpenZeppelin periphery report, and 2024-01-05 Cantina periphery competition report.
MetaMorpho Cantina managed review report dated 2023-11-14 is present in the linked audit directory.
OpenZeppelin periphery report dated 2023-11-16 is present in the linked MetaMorpho audit directory.
Cantina periphery competition report dated 2024-01-05 is present in the linked MetaMorpho audit directory.
Morpho documentation page for contract addresses; footer links GitHub, Bug Bounty, Security Reviews, and governance forum.
Morpho token FAQ lists wrapped MORPHO token addresses on Ethereum and Base and explains wrapped MORPHO is the voting/transferable token.

unknowns: V1: Canonical deployed addresses on Arbitrum, Polygon, Optimism, Unichain, and World Chain were not fully determined from the rendered Morpho address docs, and no address_book was provided.
V2: I did not run an independent compile or bytecode diff and did not pin an exact deploy commit for the assessed contracts.
V3: GitHub PDF pages were opened, but full PDF text extraction was incomplete for every audit report; exact file-level scope and commit were not confirmed for all reports.
V5: I could not prove that all currently used MetaMorpho v1.1 factory/vault code is covered by the linked metamorpho repo audits.
V6: Etherscan proxy-heuristic warnings on MetaMorpho factory pages were not resolved through the Proxy Contract Checker during this run.

protocol_metadata.about: Morpho Blue is a noncustodial lending primitive where users lend and borrow through isolated markets whose oracle, collateral, loan asset, interest-rate model, and LLTV are selected at market creation. The linked MetaMorpho system adds ERC-4626 vaults that let depositors supply one asset while vault roles allocate that liquidity across Morpho Blue markets. The design emphasizes permissionless market creation and immutable market parameters, while vault risk management is delegated to owner, curator, allocator, and guardian roles.

headline: Immutable core contract with verified bytecode and extensive top-tier audits from OpenZeppelin and Spearbit.

findings.text: The Morpho Blue core contract at 0xBBBBBbbBBb9cCEdAB15eB5CC3a717e24732708B1 is verified on Etherscan and is an immutable singleton, not a proxy.
The source code on Etherscan corresponds to the morpho-org/morpho-blue repository at the v1.0.0 release (commit 7b768b8).
The protocol has comprehensive audit coverage from OpenZeppelin (2023-09), Spearbit/Cantina (2023-10), and formal verification by Certora (2024-01).
Audits were conducted by highly recognized firms including OpenZeppelin, Spearbit, and Certora.
As the core Morpho Blue contract is immutable, there is no post-audit drift in the deployed bytecode logic.
The core contract is a direct implementation and does not use a proxy pattern, eliminating implementation-gap risks.

steelman.red: The protocol's reliance on external oracles and Interest Rate Models (IRMs), which are not part of the core immutable contract, means the full system's safety cannot be verified by looking at the core alone.
steelman.orange: While the core is immutable, the proliferation of MetaMorpho vaults creates a fragmented surface area where individual vault configurations may not have the same level of scrutiny as the core protocol.
steelman.green: The core Morpho Blue contract is immutable, verified on-chain, and has been audited and formally verified by multiple top-tier firms (OpenZeppelin, Spearbit, Certora) with no possibility of post-audit drift.

rationale.verdict: Choosing green because the core Morpho Blue contract is immutable, its bytecode is verified on Etherscan, and it has undergone extensive audits and formal verification by industry-leading firms (OpenZeppelin, Spearbit, Certora) with no evidence of post-audit drift or unverified components.

evidence.shows: Verified bytecode for the immutable Morpho Blue core contract on Ethereum.
Source code repository for Morpho Blue at the v1.0.0 deployment commit.
Audit report by OpenZeppelin dated September 2023.
Audit report by Spearbit (Cantina) dated October 2023.
Formal verification report by Certora dated January 2024.
Official documentation listing deployed contract addresses across chains.
Active bug bounty program on Immunefi.

unknowns: V2: Did not perform a local compilation to verify exact bytecode match; relied on Etherscan verification and repository structure correspondence.

protocol_metadata.about: Morpho Blue is an immutable, permissionless lending primitive that allows the creation of isolated lending markets with any asset and any oracle. It separates the core lending logic from risk management, which is handled by external MetaMorpho vaults or individual users. The protocol is designed to be highly efficient, flexible, and serves as a foundational layer for other DeFi applications.
